content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree GGG with NNN vertices numbered 111 through NNN. It is guaranteed that NNN is even.

For a positive integer kkk, let's define a graph HkH_kHk‚Äã as follows:


HkH_kHk‚Äã has NNN vertices numbered 111 through NNN.
For each edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) in GGG, there is an edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) in HkH_kHk‚Äã too.
For each pair of vertices (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) in GGG such that their distance is at most kkk, there is an edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) in HkH_kHk‚Äã.
We call a graph good if its vertices can be split into two sets UUU and VVV satisfying the following:


Each vertex appears in exactly one set; ‚à£U‚à£=‚à£V‚à£=N/2|U| = |V| = N/2‚à£U‚à£=‚à£V‚à£=N/2.
Let EEE be the set of edges (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) such that u‚ààUu \in Uu‚ààU and v‚ààVv \in Vv‚ààV. It is possible to reach any vertex from any other vertex using only the edges in EEE.
Your task is to find the minimum value of kkk such that HkH_kHk‚Äã is a good graph, and one possible way to partition vertices of this graph HkH_kHk‚Äã into the sets UUU and VVV defined above. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: the graph on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv in GGG.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print three lines.
The first line should contain a single integer ‚Äî the minimum value of kkk.
The second line should contain N/2N/2N/2 space-separated integers ‚Äî the numbers of vertices in your set UUU.
The third line should also contain N/2N/2N/2 space-separated integers ‚Äî the numbers of vertices in your set VVV.
